toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, the standard approach is to use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or the older `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump`). This tells the .NET runtime to automatically generate a dump when the process crashes.

Key environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — MiniDumpWithFullMemory (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/path/to/dump` — specifies where to write the dump file

Now I'll set up these environment variables and run the tests.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-ecd879d4c4b04771b815a465b0b8a06f && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"$PWD/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection enabled

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what the command does:

```
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$PWD/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

- **`DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1`** — tells the .NET runtime to generate a dump on crash
- **`DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4`** — captures a full memory dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump)
- **`DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=..._%p.dmp`** — sets the output path; `%p` is replaced by the process ID

If a test crashes the host process, a `.dmp` file will be written to the current directory. No crash occurred here, so no dump was generated.
